task.title: Writing Your First DITA Project
task.shortdesc: Build a complete documentation project from scratch — create three topic types, a map with keys, a conref library, and publish to HTML5.

p.b: After completing this chapter, you will be able to:

ul.li: Create concept, task, and reference topics from scratch
Build a DITA map with key definitions
Use conref to share reusable content
Build HTML5 output with sidebar navigation
DITA-OT installed and on your PATH
An XML editor configured (Setting Up Authoring Tools)
You have read the preceding Authoring DITA Topics chapter (or at least the templates section)

context.p: This is a hands-on, end-to-end tutorial. You will create a small "Getting Started" guide for a fictional product called WidgetPro. By the end, you will have a working DITA project that demonstrates all core DITA concepts.

info.codeblock: widgetpro-docs/ maps/ topics/ concepts/ tasks/ reference/ reuse/ out/

What Is <ph keyref="product-name"/>? is a widget management platform for teams.

helps you create, organize, and deploy widgets across multiple environments.

Key benefits
  • Centralized widget management
  • Multi-environment deployment
  • Version control built in
  • Role-based access control

ul.li: <!DOCTYPE concept ...> — This line tells the XML processor which topic type this file uses. The word after DOCTYPE (concept, task, or reference) must match your root element. You can copy this line from a template.

<ph keyref="product-name"/> — This is a key reference. Instead of typing "WidgetPro" directly, you reference a key defined in the map. If the product is rebranded, you change it once in the map — not in every topic.

info.p: Key learning: Notice <ph keyref="product-name"/> — this pulls the product name from the map. You never hard-code product names in topics.

info.codeblock:
Installing <ph keyref="product-name"/> Download and install on your machine.
  • Operating system: Windows 10+, macOS 12+, or Ubuntu 22+
  • 1 GB free disk space

This procedure installs using the command-line installer.

 Download the installer from . Run the installer. ./widgetpro-install.sh Verify the installation. widgetpro --version

Expected output: WidgetPro 3.0.0

is installed and ready to use.
info.p: Key learning: Notice the conref pulling a shared warning and keyref for the download URL.

ul.li: conref="../../reuse/shared-notes.dita#shared-notes/admin-warning" — A content reference. This pulls content from another file by pointing to a specific element's id. The format is filepath#topic-id/element-id. Think of it as an "include" that always stays in sync with the source.
<xref keyref="download-url"/> — A cross-reference resolved via a key. The actual URL is defined in the map, not in the topic.

info.codeblock: <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?> <!DOCTYPE reference PUBLIC "-//OASIS//DTD DITA Reference//EN" "reference.dtd"> <reference id="cli-options"> <title><ph keyref="product-name"/> CLI Options</title> <shortdesc>Command-line options for <ph keyref="product-name"/>.</shortdesc> <refbody> <section id="syntax"> <title>Syntax</title> <codeblock>widgetpro [command] [options]</codeblock> </section> <section id="commands"> <title>Commands</title> <simpletable> <sthead> <stentry>Command</stentry> <stentry>Description</stentry> </sthead> <strow> <stentry><codeph>init</codeph></stentry> <stentry>Initialize a new widget project</stentry> </strow> <strow> <stentry><codeph>build</codeph></stentry> <stentry>Build widgets for deployment</stentry> </strow> <strow> <stentry><codeph>deploy</codeph></stentry> <stentry>Deploy widgets to target environment</stentry> </strow> <strow> <stentry><codeph>--version</codeph></stentry> <stentry>Display version information</stentry> </strow> <strow> <stentry><codeph>--help</codeph></stentry> <stentry>Display help text</stentry> </strow> </simpletable> </section> </refbody> </reference>
info.p: Key learning: Reference topics are scannable. Use tables consistently for parameters, commands, and options.

info.codeblock: <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?> <!DOCTYPE topic PUBLIC "-//OASIS//DTD DITA Topic//EN" "topic.dtd"> <topic id="shared-notes"> <title>Shared Notes Library</title> <body> <note id="admin-warning" type="warning">You must have administrator privileges to perform this operation.</note> <note id="backup-reminder" type="tip">Always back up your configuration before making changes.</note> </body> </topic>
info.p: Key learning: Each reusable element has a stable id. Topics pull them with conref="reuse/shared-notes.dita#shared-notes/admin-warning".

info.codeblock:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?> <!DOCTYPE map PUBLIC "-//OASIS//DTD DITA Map//EN" "map.dtd"> <map id="widgetpro-guide"> <title>WidgetPro Getting Started Guide</title>
<!-- Key definitions — change these once, updates everywhere --> <keydef keys="product-name"> <topicmeta><keywords><keyword>WidgetPro</keyword></keywords></topicmeta> </keydef> <keydef keys="download-url" href="https://example.com/widgetpro/download" scope="external" format="html"/>
<!-- Table of contents --> <topicref href="../topics/concepts/what-is-widgetpro.dita"/> <topicref href="../topics/tasks/install-widgetpro.dita"/> <topicref href="../topics/reference/cli-options.dita"/> </map>
info.p: Key learning: The map defines product-name and download-url as keys. Topics use keyref to resolve them. To rebrand for a different product, change the map — not the topics.

info.codeblock: cd widgetpro-docs dita -i maps/widgetpro-guide.ditamap -f html5 -o out/html5 --nav-toc=full

step.cmd: Open the output and verify everything works.

substep.cmd: Verify the product name shows "WidgetPro" (from keyref).
Verify the install topic shows the admin warning (from conref).
Verify the reference topic has a formatted command table.
Verify the sidebar TOC shows all three topics.

step.cmd: Try these experiments to deepen your understanding.

p.b: Experiment A — Change the product name:

ol.li: Rebuild. Verify every page now says "GizmoMax" — without editing any topic file.

p.b: Experiment B — Add a second conref:

ol.li: Add the backup-reminder note from your reuse library to the install topic's <postreq>.
In the install topic, add two steps with platform="windows" and platform="linux".
Create a build/windows.ditaval that excludes platform="linux".
Build with --filter=build/windows.ditaval and verify only Windows content appears.

result.p: You have created a complete DITA project with three topic types, a map with key definitions, a conref reuse library, and HTML5 output. You have practiced the core DITA workflow: author → organize → build → verify.

ul.li: Concept, task, and reference topics have different structures for different purposes
Maps define the TOC structure and key definitions
Keys (keyref) make topics portable across deliverables
Conref pulls shared content from a reuse library
DITA-OT transforms XML into browsable HTML5

postreq.p: Next step: Reuse, Keys & Variant Management for advanced reuse patterns.
